solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    bool isValid(string s) {
        stack<char> st;
        unordered_map<char, char> brackets = {
            {')', '('},
            {'}', '{'},
            {']', '['}
        };

        for (char c : s) {
            if (brackets.count(c) == 0) {
                st.push(c);
            } else {
                if (st.empty() || st.top() != brackets[c]) {
                    return false;
                }
                st.pop();
            }
        }

        return st.empty();
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses a stack to efficiently track and validate the order of brackets. Each opening bracket is pushed onto the stack, and each closing bracket is checked against the top of the stack. If they match, the top is popped; otherwise, the string is invalid. The stack ensures correct bracket ordering and nesting. The time and space complexity are both O(n), where n is the length of the string.
